vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or in a state of advanced decay.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; soil appears barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; soil appears barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is not visible.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a sensor probe. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains only soil. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor and soil. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P4: Shared black pot contains only soil. No plant matter detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white, crystalline debris (likely eggshell fragments) in P3. The sensor probe in P1 is the only non-biological structural element.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: Over the 5-day sequence, there has been zero vegetative growth or recovery. The state of the biome has remained static, indicating either total plant loss or extreme dormancy.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue suggests the specimens have perished or were removed prior to the start of this sequence.
anomalies: White cylindrical object (likely a container or sensor housing) adjacent to P1
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish a baseline by mapping the registry to the physical containers. Upon validation, I observed that while the containers (P1-P4) are present, they are devoid of the expected botanical specimens. The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only identifiable object besides the soil and sensor equipment. The environment appears to be a 'dead' biome, with no evidence of growth, decay, or recovery over the 5-day observation period. The soil moisture levels appear constant, suggesting the environment is being maintained, but the biological components are absent.
